title: GNtech
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from GNtech, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Palantir Employees Are Starting to Wonder if They're the Bad Guys
items.description: Interviews with current and former Palantir employees, along with internal Slack messages obtained by WIRED, suggest a workforce in turmoil.

items.title: At 'AI Coachella,' Stanford Students Line Up to Learn From Silicon Valley Royalty
items.description: CS 153 has gone viral on the Palo Alto campus—and on X. Not everyone is happy about it.

items.title: Apple’s Next Chapter, SpaceX and Cursor Strike a Deal, and Palantir’s Controversial Manifesto
items.description: In this week’s episode of Uncanny Valley, we talk about Tim Cook’s legacy as CEO at Apple and what his long-rumored departure means for the future of ...

items.title: Lume Cube Edge Light Go Review (2026): Versatile, Portable
items.description: Portable lamps are rarely, if ever, as powerful, precise, and multipurpose as the Lume Cube Edge Light Go.

items.title: Don’t stop hiring humans — stop hiring the wrong humans, Artisan’s founder says
items.description: Artisan may be known for their bold “Stop Hiring Humans” campaign but the reality is every founder needs to assemble the right team if they want to sc...

items.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
items.description: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.

items.title: Trump’s pick to run US cyber agency CISA asks to drop out
items.description: Sean Plankey has requested to withdraw his name to run the U.S. cybersecurity agency after a tumultuous year of chaotic temporary leadership.

items.title: Instagram tests a new ‘Instants’ app for sharing disappearing photos
items.description: The app lets users share disappearing photos with their friends that can be viewed only once and remain available for 24 hours.

items.title: These New Smart Glasses From Ex-OnePlus Engineers Have a Hidden Cost

items.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI ‘super app’
items.description: OpenAI says its latest model offers increased capabilities across a broad variety of categories.
